scenario: A loan-application process has three steps logically grouped as "Identity Verification": ID check, address check, biometric check. The grouping is visual only -- the steps run sequentially in the parent flow. The team wants to collapse the group into a single visual element when not needed.
question: Which BPMN construct fits?

options.text: Call Activity referencing a separate BPMN process definition. A Call Activity invokes an externally defined child process, creating a separate process instance with its own lifecycle and isolated variable scope. Changes to the called process require separate deployment and versioning. When all steps share the same parent process variables and the grouping is purely visual, using a Call Activity adds unnecessary complexity and deployment overhead.

Annotation box drawn around the three tasks. BPMN text annotations are documentation artefacts that visually label elements with human-readable notes. They have no structural or execution semantics within the process definition; Zeebe ignores them entirely at runtime. Annotations cannot be collapsed or expanded in Modeler, so they provide no ability to hide the internal steps for readability when viewing the overall process.

Three separate Pools -- one per verification check. Pools in BPMN represent separate autonomous process participants or organisations, each with its own independent process definition and deployment unit. Using separate Pools for what are logically sequential steps within one organisation's process is structurally wrong and prevents inline sequencing, shared variable scope, and single-definition deployment required here for a unified verification workflow.

Embedded Subprocess -- groups the three identity-verification steps inline within the same parent process definition. It shares the parent's variable scope, so all process variables are accessible inside without additional mapping. Modelers can collapse it to a single element for a cleaner overview or expand it to see the internal steps. No separate deployment or child-process lifecycle management is needed.

a.text: Incorrect. Embedded Subprocess groups inline steps in the same definition, sharing parent scope. Can be collapsed for readability. Right tool for visual structuring within one process.

b.text: Incorrect. Call Activity points to a separate definition -- different lifecycle, separate variable scope.

c.text: Incorrect. Annotations don't form a structural group.
